item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im neutral on this one i don't like it but i also don't think its that bad.

item.title: "Temporal" tag?
item.content:encoded: Is there any particular reason this is tagged as "temporal", or am I just misunderstanding what the "temporal" tag means?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting premise but "at which point the walls will begin to build themselves around the subject" is just sort of confusing for me and threw me off at the end. Like do they grow? Some ghost starts stacking them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two questions:
Why is this Euclid? Seems relatively harmless and easy to contain. Nothing's trying to escape or even lure victims.

What happens when two or more eligible subjects enter? Certainly seems like something the foundation would test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: needs more testing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could be a bit more interesting with some more work put into it. For now I'm downvoting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. As someone said, I get the idea that this castle was once explicitly designed (a geas maybe?) to make a good king, a happy king, but over time as the walls crumbled, so too did the magic. But as we supply the magic with new kings, slowly it is repairing…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neatish concept, but pretty terrible execution. I would look towards a hero is born or that king arthur thing bright did for examples of conceps similar to this one but done well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted for instant crazy, certain death with no saving throw plus failure to explore what happens if you Taser the crazy and drag him off-site.

Interesting premise, though, and I do love the ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A firm "eh" from me. Not bad enough to downvote, but I don't much like it either.

Same here, except I'm starting to lean towards the 'downvote' side for articles I feel are this mediocre. So, -1'ed. It needs something more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some wording issues around the paragraph about aging, and you need to format your image caption properly, but you really got me with the final step of the whole process. Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly what I was going for. I'm glad you like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The phrase "dilapidated ruin of a castle" is a little florid for this format.

That aside, neither the concept nor its execution are bad enough to warrant a downvote, but I can't say I'm overly fond of either…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda like this one; not sure if this was what you were going for, but I envision it as a castle made for a king, and housing a long line of kings. It was happy then; eventually, the kings were overthrown, and the castle had no use, and slowly began to fall apart. So it decided, if it doesn't HAVE a king, it'll *make* a king… And it has a lot of time to catch up on, but it's already feeling better…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apart from some random culchies in the west of Ireland, but they don't have internet, so no one cares.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Irishman here, reporting in to say that you're completely right. Completely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, all of those have been fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If left on-site for extended periods,

You might want to explicitly note that the effect in permanent, even if taken of the site.

Translations of such speech have proven it to be mostly threats of violence against imagined “rival clans” and imagined scenarios of what the subject would do should any man challenge his right to be king.

So if an IRA member was placed in the SCP he wouldn't rant about throwing the English out of North Ireland?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does have promise, but there's a bunch of goofy shit dragging it down.

+ show block
– hide block

"lord of all lands as far as the eye can see,"

This requires a footnote explaining where you are quoting from. Quotes do not excuse informal language in a professional document, so explain your choice of words by mentioning whom this was quoted from.

these attempts, while potentially viewed as humorous, have occasionally resulted in fatalities and should be treated as serious assaults on Foundation staff.

No lolFoundation. Cut out the bolded portion.

SCP-1211, as seen from a nearby treetop.

If you hadn't pointed that out, I would have never pictured where the camera would have to be. Now I just think of a Foundation worker climbing a pine to snap a photo of this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get a similar impression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, but it's not grabbing me either. There's a few minor nitpicks in language usage I have which I'll throw into an edit in a sec. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A firm "eh" from me. Not bad enough to downvote, but I don't much like it either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem. The language is so dead, not even Irish people themselves bother to speak it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I'm used to the language being referred to as Gaelic, not simply 'Irish'. I apologize for my ignorance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, I'll get on revising the tone and stuff. Just for clarification purposes and so I don't look like a moron lol, Irish is a language. Here's the wikipedia page so you know I'm not making shit up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, I'm glad you started with a clean slate to a new article.

A perimeter of 50
only males age 35
After three (3)
After one (1) month

I'm confused at the number inconsistency. Either one or the other, both is unnecessary.

Any murmuring observed after this point will consist only of the Irish word for “happy” again and again.

I don't get it. I don't believe Irish is a language. Gaelic maybe?

I'm far too tired to go into a more extensive review, but the article has some tone issues. Overall, I kind of like the idea of deluding a man so the building can repair itself to its own needs (which is why I wrote 1551, lol) but there are little details that are annoying. Also, the article itself doesn't do very much to me. I don't hate it, but I don't feel anything.
